In FY26, Vedanta completed one of the largest corporate restructurings in Indian corporate history, demerging its aluminium, oil & gas, power, and iron & steel businesses into separately listed entities, leaving the remaining Vedanta Limited more narrowly focused, while it continues to hold a majority stake in separately-listed Hindustan Zinc.
moat_note: Majority ownership of Hindustan Zinc, the world's largest integrated zinc producer, remains Vedanta's core enduring asset post-demerger — a stake built over decades that would be effectively impossible to reassemble today.
name: Vedanta Limited
source_note: Vedanta FY26 & Q4 FY26 results coverage (Tickertape); Screener.in
value_chain_note: Vedanta's relationship with Hindustan Zinc is now the central value chain link for the parent company — dividends flowing up from Hindustan Zinc to Vedanta (and from Vedanta further up to Twin Star Holdings and ultimately Vedanta Resources) are a direct, quantifiable financial connection between two companies in this dataset.
value_chain_position: Following the FY26 demerger, Vedanta Limited's most significant remaining asset is its majority stake in Hindustan Zinc, alongside residual natural resources operations; the aluminium, oil & gas, power and iron & steel businesses that once made Vedanta a fully diversified conglomerate now operate as independent, separately listed companies.

business_core: Waaree Energies manufactures solar photovoltaic modules — buying or making the raw ingots, wafers and cells, assembling them into panels, and selling them to project developers, utilities, and retail rooftop customers. It is India's largest solar module manufacturer and the largest non-Chinese manufacturer globally, and is increasingly integrating backward into polysilicon, ingots, wafers, battery storage and even green hydrogen to capture more of the value chain than a pure module assembler.
moat_note: Scale and backward integration — as the largest non-Chinese solar module manufacturer with an expanding in-house supply chain from ingots to modules to batteries, Waaree captures margin at multiple stages that pure assemblers or import-dependent rivals cannot.
name: Waaree Energies
source_note: Waaree Energies FY26 & Q4 FY26 results coverage (SaurEnergy, Mercom India, SolarQuarter, ChartAlert analysis of company filings); Screener.in; Kotak Neo shareholding data
value_chain_note: Waaree's strategy is explicitly to control more of this chain rather than just sell modules — recent moves into PV glass, transformers, battery cells and green hydrogen electrolysers aim to capture value at multiple stages rather than compete purely on module price.
value_chain_position: Sits upstream in the solar value chain as a manufacturer — Waaree makes the ingots, wafers, cells and modules that project developers like Adani Green then buy and install, and is now integrating even further upstream into polysilicon sourcing and battery storage.

business_core: Wipro is India's fourth-largest IT services company, providing software development, consulting and outsourced technology operations to global enterprise clients on the same billing model as larger peers TCS, Infosys and HCLTech. Growth momentum has lagged those larger peers for several years, and the company is currently emphasizing cost discipline and shareholder capital returns (its first buyback in three years) over aggressive growth reinvestment.
moat_note: Scale and decades of enterprise relationships provide some baseline moat, but Wipro's multi-year growth underperformance relative to TCS, Infosys and HCLTech suggests its competitive position within the traditional staffing-pyramid IT services model has eroded more than peers'. The current strategic response — cost discipline plus capital returns — is a defensible reaction to that weaker position, not evidence the underlying moat has itself strengthened.
name: Wipro
source_note: Wipro's own Q4 & FY26 results release (wipro.com), cross-checked against MarketsMojo and Outlook Business coverage
value_chain_note: The shift toward cost discipline and shareholder returns (buyback plus dividends) over aggressive reinvestment is a reasonable response to that weaker growth position, but also a tacit signal that management sees limited near-term high-return growth opportunities to deploy capital into instead.
value_chain_position: The same downstream IT-services value-chain position as TCS, Infosys and HCLTech — no physical raw-material chain, competing on delivery cost, quality and increasingly AI-augmented productivity within client-defined project scope — though Wipro currently occupies a distinctly weaker growth position within that peer group.

business_core: Zydus Lifesciences develops, manufactures and markets pharmaceutical formulations across North America generics, India branded formulations, international markets, and a growing biosimilars pipeline, alongside a distinct Consumer Wellness division selling market-leading over-the-counter brands (Glucon-D, Nycil, Sugar Free, Complan, Nutralite). It is one of India's most diversified pharmaceutical and consumer health companies, ranking 3rd among US generic companies by prescription volume.
moat_note: Rare diversification across prescription generics, branded formulations, biosimilars and market-leading consumer wellness brands (Glucon-D, Sugar Free, Nycil all rank #1 in their categories) — a breadth of moats across genuinely different businesses that few pure pharmaceutical peers can match.
name: Zydus Lifesciences
source_note: Zydus Lifesciences FY26 & Q4 FY26 results coverage (Business Standard, Groww, InvestyWise); Screener.in
value_chain_note: Zydus's Consumer Wellness segment (Glucon-D, Nycil, Sugar Free, Complan, Nutralite) is an unusual feature among pure pharmaceutical companies in this dataset — it gives Zydus a direct-to-consumer branded goods business layered on top of its core prescription drug manufacturing, diversifying its value chain position beyond a typical pharma company's B2B-heavy model.
value_chain_position: Zydus Lifesciences develops, manufactures and sells pharmaceutical formulations across generics (primarily North America), branded formulations (India), consumer wellness products, and international markets, alongside an increasingly important biosimilars pipeline — a diversified pharmaceutical and consumer health company spanning both prescription and over-the-counter products.
